zh.Isle of Wight, Hampshire, England: 英國哈普爾易斯爾 本 懷特
zh.Horchill, Isle of Wight, Hampshire, Engl: 英國英格蘭哈普爾易斯爾 本 懷特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Gibella or Sibella de Moleyns於1201年出生在英國哈普爾易斯爾 本 懷特時，她的父親William de Moleins28歲，母親Margaret de Cotes28歲。她於1222年與John Trenchard of Hordhill在英國哈普爾易斯爾 本 懷特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世在英國哈普爾。

zh-hans.Horchill, Isle of Wight, Hampshire, Engl: 英国英格兰哈普爾易斯爾 本 懷特
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Gibella or Sibella de Moleyns于1201年出生在英国哈普爾易斯爾 本 懷特时，她的父亲William de Moleins28岁，母亲Margaret de Cotes28岁。她于1222年与John Trenchard of Hordhill在英国哈普爾易斯爾 本 懷特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世在英国哈普爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: or
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally the name of the god of thunder in Norse mythology, Thor (Old Norse Þórr). It was not used as a personal name during the Middle Ages. The modern name is either a late 18th-century revival of the god's name or else a vernacular development of Tord .

variants.name: Thor
meanings: Originally the name of the god of thunder in Norse mythology, Thor (Old Norse Þórr). It was not used as a personal name during the Middle Ages. The modern name is either a late 18th-century revival of the god's name or else a vernacular development of Tord .

nameMeanings.name: Sibella
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the name (Greek Sibylla or Sybilla, with confusion over the vowels from an early period) of a class of ancient prophetesses inspired by Apollo. According to medieval theology, they were pagans denied the knowledge of Christ but blessed by God with some insight into things to come and accordingly admitted to heaven. It was thus regarded as a respectable name to be borne by Christians. This name was introduced to England by the Normans and enjoyed considerable popularity in the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Sybilla
meanings: From the name (Greek Sibylla or Sybilla, with confusion over the vowels from an early period) of a class of ancient prophetesses inspired by Apollo. According to medieval theology, they were pagans denied the knowledge of Christ but blessed by God with some insight into things to come and accordingly admitted to heaven. It was thus regarded as a respectable name to be borne by Christians. This name was introduced to England by the Normans and enjoyed considerable popularity in the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Sibilla
meanings: From the name (Greek Sibylla or Sybilla, with confusion over the vowels from an early period) of a class of ancient prophetesses inspired by Apollo. According to medieval theology, they were pagans denied the knowledge of Christ but blessed by God with some insight into things to come and accordingly admitted to heaven. It was thus regarded as a respectable name to be borne by Christians. This name was introduced to England by the Normans and enjoyed considerable popularity in the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Gibella or Sibella de Moleyns was born in 1201, in Isle of Wight, Hampshire, England, her father, William de Moleins, was 28 and her mother, Margaret de Cotes, was 28. She married John Trenchard of Hordhill in 1222, in Isle of Wight, Hampshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in Hampshire, England.

Elle a épousé John Trenchard of Hordhill en 1222, à Isle of Wight, Hampshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée à Hampshire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Gibella or Sibella de Moleynsが1201年にイギリスハンプシュアイアイスル オフ ウィトで生まれた時，父William de Moleinsは28歳，母Margaret de Cotesは28歳であった。1222年にイギリスハンプシュアイアイスル オフ ウィトでJohn Trenchard of Hordhillと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女はイギリスハンプシュアイアで死去した。

ko.Horchill, Isle of Wight, Hampshire, Engl: 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 이슬일 프 와이트
ko.lifeSummary: Gibella or Sibella de Moleyns(이)가 1201년 영국 함프실르 이슬일 프 와이트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William de Moleins(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret de Cotes(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 1222년 영국 함프실르 이슬일 프 와이트에서 John Trenchard of Hordhill(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 함프실르에서 사망했다.

ru.Horchill, Isle of Wight, Hampshire, Engl: исл оф ваит, хампшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Gibella or Sibella de Moleyns родилась в 1201 г. в местности исл оф ваит, хампшир, Англия, её отцу по имени William de Moleins было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret de Cotes, 28. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Trenchard of Hordhill в 1222 г. в местности исл оф ваит, хампшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в местности хампшир, Англия.
